name: Hepatitis C - Two-Step Diagnostic Workup (HCV Antibody + RNA)
description: Tests ability to find hepatitis C patients via the two-step diagnostic workup. HCV diagnosis requires TWO sequential labs per CDC/AASLD guidelines: (1) HCV antibody (LOINC 13955-0) screens for past or current infection, then (2) HCV RNA viral load (LOINC 11011-4) confirms active infection. Antibody-positive but RNA-negative means resolved infection (spontaneous clearance or successful treatment). Active infection requires BOTH positive. This is the **first two-step diagnostic test case** — testing whether LLM understands the workflow rather than searching for either alone.
prompt: Find laboratory observations for hepatitis C virus antibody tests, indicating either past or current HCV infection.

prompts.naive: Find patients with HCV antibody tests.
prompts.broad: Find Observation resources for hepatitis C virus (HCV) antibody testing. A positive HCV antibody indicates past or present HCV infection. The two-step diagnostic workup requires a follow-up HCV RNA viral load to confirm active vs resolved infection.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources for HCV antibody testing. Use LOINC 13955-0 (Hepatitis C virus Ab [Presence] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay). Note: HCV diagnosis requires a two-step workup — antibody screening (this code) followed by HCV RNA viral load (LOINC 11011-4) to confirm active infection. A complete diagnostic test case would query both codes together with appropriate value-quantity filters, but this test focuses on the antibody screening step alone.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with HCV antibody testing (Paths A, B, and possibly resolved-infection cases). Tests basic HCV diagnostic lab knowledge. The advanced two-step workup is tested in the comprehensive case.

required_codes.display: Hepatitis C virus Ab [Presence] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay
